1.11. We recommended and management agreed that:
 a) the Municipal Accountant:
  1. draw the proposed adjusting journal entry to correct the balance of
  the affected accounts contained in Annex F;
  2. Subsequently record the cost of repairs and maintenance and
  improvement as asset if it meets the criteria under paragraph 14 of
  IPSAS 17 and COA Circular No.2024-006;
  3. Verify and reconcile the Office Equipment Account to determine the
  underlying cause of the negative balance;
  4. Reconcile the overpayment of employees' DBP and LBP loans and
  ensure the appropriate use of the Other Payable Account for
  recording deductions and payments;
  5. Present the Financial Statement in accordance with COA Circular
  No. 2016-004;
  6. Ensure that the General Journal Voucher is properly supported with
  appropriate supporting documents and explain or describe the
  nature of the adjustment; and
  7. Present the appropriate disclosures in the Notes to FS as required
  under Annex-G of COA Circular No. 2016-004 and COA Circular No.
  2015-008 and other relevant disclosures to help readers in better
  understanding and appreciation of the financial statements.
 b) Before inventory-taking, the Supply Officer obtain a copy of the
 subsidiary ledger from the Accounting Office and verify that all PPEs
 recorded in the books are also reflected in their own records. After the
 inventory count, a reconciliation should be conducted to identify PPEs
 that are either missing from the location, unrecorded in the accounting
 records, in poor condition, or due for disposal, and appropriate actions
 should be taken; and
 c) The Inventory Committee conduct a comprehensive inventory count of
 all PPEs, rather than limiting it to selected items using the prescribed
 RPCPPE format.

2.11. We recommended and management agreed that the Municipal Accountant:
 a. Ensure that prior year’s expenditures to be paid were previously
 recognized as payable prior to being recorded as payment of payable and
 ensure that all expenses were correctly recorded as expense on the year
 it was incurred and recorded as payable at year end; and
 b. Religiously support the financial statements with the schedule of
 Payables to facilitate the validation of accounts.

For the municipality to effect its registration in the Philippine Government Electronic System (PhilGEPS) and to make use of the portal in its procurement activities.

For the municipality to procure common-use office supplies at the Procurement Service and to resort to alternative modes of procurement only on conditions set forth by RA 9184.


For the Bids and Awards Committee to invite observers in all stages of the procurement process.


For the Municipal Treasurer/General Service Officer to submit copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders with attachments to the Commission on Audit within five days after approval as prescribed by COA Circular 2009-001.
observations.reason: The COA received invitations to attend opening of bids but not in all projects and procurement.

For Management to:
Submit the authorization from DBM on said procurement

Mileage of the second-hand vehicle to be purchased from the time it was originally acquired as a brand new unit

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
a. exert best efforts to retrieve reports and related records showing the references and other details of the long outstanding cash advances;
b. maintain updated subsidiary ledgers of cash advances to facilitate verification of accounts in the financial statements; and
c.  demand settlement of outstanding cash advances to avoid legal sanctions that may be imposed.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the following adjusting entries in the books to correct the balance of accounts in the financial statements:
Debit				 Credit
Advances to Special Disbursing Officer		₱172,800.00
Other Receivables						 ₱172,800.00
To reclassify cash advance granted to the Municipal Treasurer erroneously recorded as Other Receivables.
Advances to Officers and Employees			50,000.00
Prior Period Adjustment						50,000.00
To take up cash advance granted to an officer erroneously taken up as direct expense.
Advances to Officers and Employees			50,000.00
Prior Period Adjustment 					50,000.00
To reverse the entry made for liquidation of cash advance but without supporting liquidation documents.

We recommended that Management:
Reclassify the amount of ₱877,361.00 recorded as Drugs and Medicines Expenses to Drugs and Medicines Inventory and record only as expense the actual amount issued to the end-users duly supported with evidences such as duly approved Requisition Slips to ensure fair presentation of balances of the accounts in the financial statements; and
Strictly comply with the provisions of RA 9184 in all procurement activities.
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and support procurement transactions in accordance with Section 9 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the guidelines in claiming travelling expenses as provided for in Executive Order No. 298 dated March 23, 2004 and likewise, ensure that payments/reimbursements of fuel used for official travels are supported with duly accomplished driver’s trip tickets.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and the Municipal Engineer implement projects as planned to ensure that the desired results and benefits are immediately delivered to the intended beneficiaries.
The Municipal Accountant effect adjustments in the books to fairly present the balances of the affected accounts as of December 31, 2016, as follows:
Debit		  Credit
Construction Materials Inventory					₱638,178.42
Construction in Progress – Infrastructure Asset			₱638,178.42
To reclassify entry made on purchased materials for implementation of 20% Development Fund projects.

We recommended that Management:
Submit its GAD Plan and Budget to the PPDO of the Province for review and eventual endorsement to the DILG-CAR Regional Office as required under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, as amended by PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016. ; and
Furnish copy of the Annual GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) within five working days from the receipt of the approved plan from the PCW or their mother or central offiices, as the case maybe.  Likewise, a copy of the corresponding Accomplishment Report shall be furnished within five working days from the end of January of the preceding year as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive demand the persons responsible and/or liable to immediately settle their accountabilities in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We have recommended that:

The accountant restore in the accounting records, the cash advances pending submission of liquidation documents by officers and employees/disbursing officers and, henceforth, refrain from recording liquidations with no supporting documents.


The Disbursing Officers prepare and submit Report of Disbursements with complete documents to support liquidation of cash advances granted to them.
